Items.Description:
一旦被暂停，Ben 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

取消暂停后，本将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ben 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ben 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 ben 的操作：

降低 ben 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning ben 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, ben will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ben will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ben:

Make all posts by ben less visible

ben consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts.
Items.Author: ben

Items.Title: GPT-4 和我们 4 个人一起玩 DND，这太疯狂了🤯 | GPT-4 plays DND with 4 of us, it's insane 🤯
Items.Description:
迪马·苏哈列夫

发表于 4 月 25 日

它会让你大吃一惊

这是 GPT-4 扮演地下城主的角色，有我和我的 3 个朋友参加

我会把这个东西编码成一个网络游戏，这样你就不用为团队联系电话而烦恼了；）

转发推文让更多人知道它并关注我成为第一个知道游戏何时公开的人

你的四位英雄——Max Voltage、Cassie Chroma、Rex Rocket 和 Maxine Flash——发现自己身处繁华的创新和创造力中心城市 Neonopolis。 当您穿过城市街道时，您会注意到一个全息广告牌显示一条消息：“需要帮助：神秘的停电一直困扰着 Neonopolis。Synthia 市长正在寻找熟练的冒险家来调查和解决这个问题。奖励：5000 代币和一件稀有神器。 “

建议的行动：

  1. 前往 Synthia 市长办公室接受任务（DC：0）
  2. 从当地人那里收集有关停电的信息（DC：8）
  3. 参观附近的拱廊以放松身心并收集您的想法（DC：0）

脉冲手枪——一种发射集中能量脉冲的紧凑型能量武器。 紧急降落伞——用于紧急着陆的紧凑型塑料降落伞。 当您进入时尚、现代的建筑时，友好的接待员会迎接您，并带您前往市长办公室。 建议的行动：

  1. 向 Synthia 市长询问有关停电的任何线索或信息 (DC: 0)
  2. 提议巡逻城市并寻找任何可疑活动（DC：10）
  3. 建议分开以覆盖更多地面并调查不同区域（DC：0）

一行人准备在 Neonopolis 巡逻，留意任何异常情况。

该党注意到一群阴暗的人潜伏在变电站附近，摆弄一些设备。 他们似乎在篡改城市的电网。

建议的行动：

  1. 面对阴暗的人并质疑他们的意图（DC：0）
  2. 从远处观察并收集更多信息（DC：8）
  3. 立即向 Synthia 市长报告可疑活动 (DC: 0)

当你接近时，他们会注意到你的存在并准备好自卫。

请掷 d20 以检查您的脉冲手枪是否成功命中（DC：11）。 如果成功，则掷 d6 以确定造成的伤害。

他们会迅速对您的攻击做出反应并准备反击。 其中一个拿出能量武器对准雷克斯。

如果不成功，则掷 d6 以确定受到的伤害。

能量武器击中雷克斯，造成伤害。 请掷 d6 以确定受到的伤害。

局势已经升级为一场全面的战斗场景，阴暗的人现在意识到你的存在并准备战斗。

建议的行动：

  1. 试图逃离战斗并重新集结（DC：12）

涂鸦无人机——一种用于标记和侦察的小型遥控无人机。 数字钥匙 — 用于进入受限区域的通用塑料钥匙。 请掷 d20 来检查 Arc Disruptor（DC：12）是否成功命中。 如果成功，则掷 d6 以确定造成的伤害。

掷 d6 并将结果加倍以确定由于致命一击造成的伤害。

强大的放电击垮了他们，导致他们倒在地上，失去了行动能力。 随着直接威胁被消除，该党现在可以决定他们的下一步行动方案。

建议的行动：

  1. 审问丧失行为能力的可疑人员（DC：0）
  2. 搜索该区域以寻找与停电有关的任何线索或证据 (DC: 8)
  3. 向 Synthia 市长报告你的发现 (DC: 0)

请掷 d20 来检查是否成功找到有用的战利品（DC：8）。

在他们的财产中，您发现了一个似乎是电网遥控器的小型电子设备，以及一张说明如何破坏城市电力供应的便条。 看来这些人确实对 Neonopolis 的停电负责。

建议的行动：

  1. 检查遥控器并尝试扭转破坏（DC：10）
  2. 将纸条和遥控器交给辛西娅市长作为证据（DC：0）
  3. 继续调查该区域以寻找任何其他线索（DC：8）

请掷 d20 来检查是否成功扭转了破坏（DC：10）。

该设备会产生火花并变得无法运行，因此无法使用它来修复电网。 该党将需要找到另一种方法来解决停电问题。

建议的行动：

  1. 把坏掉的遥控器拿去给 Synthia 市长作为证据（DC：0）
  2. 搜索该区域以寻找修复电网的任何替代方法（DC：10）
  3. 重组并讨论新的行动计划（DC：0）

请掷 d20 来检查是否成功修复设备（DC：12）。

该设备现在又能正常工作了，派对可以用它来尝试扭转对城市电网的破坏。

建议的行动：

  1. 尝试使用修好的遥控器扭转破坏（DC：10）
  2. 拿走遥控器并给 Synthia 市长做笔记作为证据（DC：0）
  3. 继续调查该区域以寻找任何其他线索（DC：8）

请掷 d20 来检查是否成功扭转了破坏（DC：10）。

影响 Neonopolis 的停电事件结束，城市的能源供应恢复正常。 该党已经解决了这个问题，现在可以将他们的发现报告给 Synthia 市长。

建议的行动：

  1. 返回 Synthia 市长办公室并出示证据（DC：0）
  2. 继续调查该区域以寻找任何其他线索（DC：8）
  3. 在附近的拱廊庆祝您的成功（DC：0）

在出示证据并解释他们如何制止这些阴暗的人后，Synthia 市长对你们的团队合作和技能表示感谢和钦佩。

正如所承诺的那样，她用 5000 代币和一件稀有神器奖励派对——太阳能放大器，一种可以增强能量武器和技能威力的装置。 一行人顺利完成了任务，获得了宝贵的经验。

恭喜你完成任务！│

热闹的气氛中充斥着讨价还价的声音、欢笑声和创新科技的嗡嗡声。 使用您新获得的代币，您可以探索市场并购买物品以增强您的能力，或者只是享受 Retrotopia 的风景和声音。

建议的行动：

  1. 浏览新武器或装备的市场（DC：0）
  2. 寻找销售稀有或独特物品的商人（DC：8）
  3. 与当地人互动并了解更多关于这座城市的信息 (DC: 0)

太阳能增幅器是一种稀有神器，可以增强基于能量的武器和技能的威力。 使用时，它会增强您的攻击或能力的有效性，使它们更强大并增加成功的机会。 当太阳能放大器被激活时，GM 可以在游戏过程中确定确切的机制和 DC 调整。

要找到愿意为稀有神器支付合理价格的买家，请掷 d20 以检查是否成功谈判一笔好交易（DC：10）。

经过一番协商，买家同意为这件稀有神器支付 3000 代币。 派对的令牌余额增加，为未来的购买和冒险提供更多资源。

当前代币余额：8000 个代币

建议的行动：

  1. 浏览新武器或装备的市场（DC：0）
  2. 寻找销售稀有或独特物品的商人（DC：8）
  3. 与当地人互动并了解更多关于这座城市的信息 (DC: 0)

当你在市场闲逛时，你会遇到三件引起你注意的有趣物品：

  1. 光子护盾：一种轻型、基于能量的护盾，可以吸收即将到来的攻击。 全息伴侣：一种可定制的小型全息宠物，可以协助完成各种任务并提供情感支持。 您可以选择购买任何这些项目或继续浏览市场。

    建议的行动：

    1. 购买光子护盾（成本：2500 代币）
    2. 购买 Sonic Boombox（成本：3000 代币）
    3. 购买全息伴侣（成本：1500 代币）
    4. 继续浏览市场寻找其他物品 (DC: 0)

    请掷 d20 来检查是否成功偷了物品而没有被抓住（DC：14）。 请记住，失败可能会导致后果。

    不仅商家会当场抓住你，你的行为也会引起附近保安人员的注意。 警卫逮捕了你和其他人，你被押送到拘留区等待进一步的后果。

    建议的行动：

    1. 尝试通过谈话摆脱困境（DC：15）
    2. 尝试逃离等候区（DC：12）
    3. 等待当局决定你的命运（DC：0）

    导电服——一种由高级塑料纤维制成的套装，可增强基于电的能力。 静电靴——产生电荷以增强运动和跳跃的靴子。 等离子手榴弹 — 充满挥发性等离子能量的球形塑料手榴弹。 能量电容器 — 用于为各种电子设备充电的便携式电池。 请掷 d20 来检查是否成功避免电力过载和逃脱（DC：11）。 请记住，您的行为可能会对您和其余党员产生影响。

    当你逃跑时，保安人员措手不及，无法及时做出反应。

    与此同时，剩下的党员将面临盗窃未遂的后果。 情况变得更加复杂，现在党内出现分裂。

    针对最大电压的建议操作：

    1. 保持低调，等待局势平静下来（DC：0）
    2. 尝试想办法从远处帮助剩余的党员（DC：10）
    3. 继续自行探索 Neonopolis（DC：0）

    对其余党员的建议行动：

    1. 尝试通过谈话摆脱困境（DC：15）
    2. 尝试逃离等候区（DC：12）
    3. 等待当局决定你的命运（DC：0）

    片刻之后，Max 在试图拯救他的队伍时死于 0/20 HP。

    我知道这很疯狂，我现在正在制作在线模式，您可以在其中与朋友一起玩

    你的战利品以及令牌和 XP 将被保存，将会有更多的世界

    转发推文让更多人知道它并关注我成为第一个知道游戏何时公开的人

    想出最好的游戏功能的人——第一个月免费❤️

    一旦暂停，disukharev 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，disukharev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，disukharev 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 disukharev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 disukharev：

    降低 disukharev 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

取消标记 disukharev 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Dima Sukharev

Posted on Apr 25

   

its gonna blow your head off

here is the GPT-4 acting as a Dungeon Master with a party of me and 3 of my friends

i will code this thing a web Game, so you are not bothered with drawing on Team Bonding calls ;)

repost the twit to tell more people about it and follow me to be the first to know when The Game is public

GPT-4: Welcome to Retrotopia, a realm where the past and future collide in a vibrant, neon-lit landscape. Your party of four heroes—Max Voltage, Cassie Chroma, Rex Rocket, and Maxine Flash—find themselves in the bustling city of Neonopolis, a hub of innovation and creativity. As you walk through the city streets, you notice a holographic billboard displaying a message: "Help Wanted: Mysterious power outages have been plaguing Neonopolis. Mayor Synthia seeks skilled adventurers to investigate and resolve the issue. Reward: 5000 Tokens and a rare artifact."

Suggested Actions:

  1. Head to Mayor Synthia's office to accept the quest (DC: 0)
  2. Gather information from the locals about the power outages (DC: 8)
  3. Visit a nearby arcade to relax and gather your thoughts (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Rex — what is my inventory? │

◇

GPT-4: GPT-4: Rex Rocket, as a Jetpack Daredevil, your inventory includes:

  1. Rocket Blasters — A set of powerful jet thrusters for high-speed aerial maneuvers. DC: 12 to check perform complex aerial stunts.
  2. Aerodynamic Armor — Lightweight plastic armor designed for optimal flight performance. DC: 10 to check adjust flight stability.
  3. Pulse Pistol — A compact energy weapon that fires concentrated energy pulses. DC: 11 to check shoot moving targets.
  4. Retrotopia Map — A holographic map of the entire Retrotopia landscape. DC: 9 to check locate hidden areas.
  5. Emergency Parachute — A compact plastic parachute for emergency landings. DC: 8 to check deploy in time.

◇ What do you do? │ Max — we head to Syntia's office │

◇

GPT-4: The party makes their way to Mayor Synthia's office, located in the heart of Neonopolis. As you enter the sleek, modern building, you're greeted by a friendly receptionist who directs you to the mayor's office. Mayor Synthia, a charismatic woman with a passion for technology, welcomes you and explains the situation: "The power outages have been happening at random, and we can't seem to find the cause. We suspect it might be sabotage, but we need your help to investigate and put an end to it."

Suggested Actions:

  1. Ask Mayor Synthia for any leads or information about the power outages (DC: 0)
  2. Offer to patrol the city and look for any suspicious activity (DC: 10)
  3. Suggest splitting up to cover more ground and investigate different areas (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Cassie — we offer to patrol the city │

◇

GPT-4: Cassie Chroma speaks up and offers to patrol the city in search of any suspicious activity related to the power outages. Mayor Synthia appreciates your initiative and agrees to the plan. The party prepares to patrol Neonopolis, keeping an eye out for anything unusual.

Cassie, please roll a d20 to check for success in finding suspicious activity (DC: 10).

◇ What do you do? │ 14 │

◇

GPT-4: With a roll of 14, Cassie successfully spots some suspicious activity while patrolling the city. The party notices a group of shady individuals lurking near a power substation, fiddling with some equipment. They seem to be tampering with the city's power grid.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Confront the shady individuals and question their intentions (DC: 0)
  2. Observe from a distance and gather more information (DC: 8)
  3. Report the suspicious activity to Mayor Synthia immediately (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Rex — i fly to them and attack │

◇

GPT-4: Rex Rocket activates his Rocket Blasters and flies towards the shady individuals, preparing to attack. As you approach, they notice your presence and get ready to defend themselves.

Please roll a d20 to check for a successful hit with your Pulse Pistol (DC: 11). If successful, roll a d6 to determine the damage dealt.

◇ What do you do? │ 7 │

◇

GPT-4: With a roll of 7, Rex Rocket's attack misses the shady individuals. They quickly react to your assault and prepare to counterattack. One of them pulls out an energy weapon and aims it at Rex.

Rex, please roll a d20 to check if you can dodge the incoming attack (DC: 11). If unsuccessful, roll a d6 to determine the damage taken.

◇ What do you do? │ 4 │

◇

GPT-4: With a roll of 4, Rex Rocket fails to dodge the incoming attack. The energy weapon hits Rex, dealing damage. Please roll a d6 to determine the damage taken.

◇ What do you do? │ 4 │

◇

GPT-4: Rex Rocket takes 4 points of damage from the energy weapon, reducing his Health Points (HP) to 16/20. The situation has escalated into a full-blown combat scenario, with the shady individuals now aware of your presence and ready to fight.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Max Voltage uses his Arc Disruptor to attack the shady individuals (DC: 12)
  2. Cassie Chroma deploys her Graffiti Drone for reconnaissance and support (DC: 9)
  3. Maxine Flash casts a spell from her Cassette Grimoire to aid the party (DC: 10)
  4. Attempt to escape the combat and regroup (DC: 12)

◇ What do you do? │ Cassie — what is my inventory? │

◇

GPT-4: Cassie Chroma, as a Holo-Hacker, your inventory includes:

  1. Holo-Deck — A device capable of creating realistic holograms and illusions. DC: 12 to check deceive targets.
  2. Cyber Shades — High-tech sunglasses that provide enhanced vision and hacking capabilities. DC: 10 to check bypass security systems.
  3. Vibro-Dagger — A retractable blade with a vibrating edge for increased cutting power. DC: 11 to check perform precise cuts.
  4. Graffiti Drone — A small remote-controlled drone used for tagging and reconnaissance. DC: 9 to check pilot accurately.
  5. Digital Key — A universal plastic key for accessing restricted areas. DC: 8 to check adapt to various lock types.

◇ What do you do? │ Max — I run into the fight to help Rex │

◇

GPT-4: Max Voltage rushes into the fight to assist Rex Rocket, preparing to use his Arc Disruptor against the shady individuals. Please roll a d20 to check for a successful hit with the Arc Disruptor (DC: 12). If successful, roll a d6 to determine the damage dealt.

◇ What do you do? │ 20 │

◇

GPT-4: With a critical roll of 20, Max Voltage expertly aims his Arc Disruptor and unleashes a powerful electrical discharge at the shady individuals. Roll a d6 and double the result to determine the damage dealt due to the critical hit.

◇ What do you do? │ 5 │

◇

GPT-4: Max Voltage deals 10 points of damage (5 doubled) to the shady individuals with his Arc Disruptor. The powerful electrical discharge overwhelms them, causing them to collapse to the ground, incapacitated. With the immediate threat neutralized, the party can now decide their next course of action.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Interrogate the incapacitated shady individuals (DC: 0)
  2. Search the area for any clues or evidence related to the power outages (DC: 8)
  3. Report back to Mayor Synthia with your findings (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Maxine — i loot them │

◇

GPT-4: Maxine Flash searches the incapacitated shady individuals for any valuable items or information. Please roll a d20 to check for success in finding useful loot (DC: 8).

◇ What do you do? │ 16 │

◇

GPT-4: With a roll of 16, Maxine Flash successfully finds some valuable loot on the shady individuals. Among their possessions, you discover a small electronic device that appears to be a remote control for the power grid, as well as a note with instructions to sabotage the city's power supply. It seems these individuals were indeed responsible for the power outages in Neonopolis.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Examine the remote control and attempt to reverse the sabotage (DC: 10)
  2. Take the note and remote control to Mayor Synthia as evidence (DC: 0)
  3. Continue investigating the area for any additional clues (DC: 8)

◇ What do you do? │ Maxine — i try to reverse the sabotage with the remote control │

◇

GPT-4: Maxine Flash attempts to use the remote control to reverse the sabotage on the city's power grid. Please roll a d20 to check for success in reversing the sabotage (DC: 10).

◇ What do you do? │ 1 │

◇

GPT-4: With a critical failure roll of 1, Maxine Flash accidentally triggers a short circuit in the remote control while trying to reverse the sabotage. The device sparks and becomes inoperable, making it impossible to use it to fix the power grid. The party will need to find another way to resolve the power outage issue.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Take the broken remote control and note to Mayor Synthia as evidence (DC: 0)
  2. Search the area for any alternative ways to fix the power grid (DC: 10)
  3. Regroup and discuss a new plan of action (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Cassie — as a holo-hacker i try to fix the remote control │

◇

GPT-4: Cassie Chroma, with her expertise as a Holo-Hacker, attempts to repair the broken remote control. Please roll a d20 to check for success in fixing the device (DC: 12).

◇ What do you do? │ 15 │

◇

GPT-4: With a roll of 15, Cassie Chroma successfully repairs the remote control. The device is now functional again, and the party can use it to try and reverse the sabotage on the city's power grid.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Attempt to reverse the sabotage using the repaired remote control (DC: 10)
  2. Take the remote control and note to Mayor Synthia as evidence (DC: 0)
  3. Continue investigating the area for any additional clues (DC: 8)

◇ What do you do? │ Max — i try to reverse the sabotage again │

◇

GPT-4: Maxine Flash, with the repaired remote control in hand, attempts once more to reverse the sabotage on the city's power grid. Please roll a d20 to check for success in reversing the sabotage (DC: 10).

◇ What do you do? │ 17 │

◇

GPT-4: With a roll of 17, Maxine Flash successfully uses the repaired remote control to reverse the sabotage on the city's power grid. The power outages affecting Neonopolis come to an end, and the city's energy supply returns to normal. The party has resolved the issue and can now report back to Mayor Synthia with their findings.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Return to Mayor Synthia's office and present the evidence (DC: 0)
  2. Continue investigating the area for any additional clues (DC: 8)
  3. Celebrate your success at a nearby arcade (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Rex — we go back to Mayor │

◇

GPT-4: The party returns to Mayor Synthia's office, eager to share their findings and success in resolving the power outage issue. Upon presenting the evidence and explaining how they stopped the shady individuals, Mayor Synthia expresses her gratitude and admiration for your teamwork and skills.

As promised, she rewards the party with 5000 Tokens and a rare artifact—a Solar Amplifier, a device that can boost the power of energy-based weapons and abilities. The party has successfully completed the quest and gained valuable experience.

Congratulations on completing the quest!│

◇ What do you do? │ Rex — we go to city market │

◇

GPT-4: The party heads to the bustling city market of Neonopolis, where merchants and inventors showcase their latest gadgets, gizmos, and retro-futuristic wares. The lively atmosphere is filled with the sounds of haggling, laughter, and the hum of innovative technology. With your newly acquired Tokens, you can explore the market and purchase items to enhance your abilities or simply enjoy the sights and sounds of Retrotopia.

  1. Browse the market for new weapons or equipment (DC: 0)
  2. Look for a merchant selling rare or unique items (DC: 8)
  3. Engage with the locals and learn more about the city (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Maxine — how much does Solar Amplifier cost and what does it do? │

◇

GPT-4: The Solar Amplifier was given to your party as a reward for completing the quest, so it is already in your possession and does not have a cost. The Solar Amplifier is a rare artifact that can boost the power of energy-based weapons and abilities. When used, it enhances the effectiveness of your attacks or abilities, making them more potent and increasing their chances of success. The exact mechanics and DC adjustments can be determined by the GM during gameplay when the Solar Amplifier is activated.

◇ What do you do? │ Maxine — i want to sell it │

◇

GPT-4: Maxine Flash decides to sell the Solar Amplifier at the city market. To find a buyer willing to pay a fair price for the rare artifact, please roll a d20 to check for success in negotiating a good deal (DC: 10).

◇ What do you do? │ 17 │

◇

GPT-4: With a roll of 17, Maxine Flash successfully finds a buyer who recognizes the value of the Solar Amplifier. After some negotiation, the buyer agrees to pay 3000 Tokens for the rare artifact. The party's Token balance increases, providing more resources for future purchases and adventures.

Current Token balance: 8000 Tokens

  3. Engage with the locals and learn more about the city (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Max — we brouse the market for cool stuff │

◇

GPT-4: The party browses the Neonopolis city market, exploring the various stalls and merchants offering a wide array of retro-futuristic items, gadgets, and equipment. As you wander through the market, you come across three interesting items that catch your attention:

  1. Photon Shield: A lightweight, energy-based shield that can absorb incoming attacks. DC: 10 to check activate/deactivate effectively.
  2. Sonic Boombox: A portable sound system that can emit powerful sonic waves, disorienting enemies. DC: 12 to check control wave intensity.
  3. Holo-Companion: A small, customizable holographic pet that can assist in various tasks and provide emotional support. DC: 8 to check program and command accurately.

You can choose to purchase any of these items or continue browsing the market.

Suggested Actions:

  3. Purchase the Holo-Companion (Cost: 1500 Tokens)
  4. Continue browsing the market for other items (DC: 0)

◇ What do you do? │ Rex — i try to steal the Boombox │

◇

GPT-4: Rex Rocket, feeling daring, attempts to steal the Sonic Boombox from the merchant's stall. Please roll a d20 to check for success in stealing the item without getting caught (DC: 14). Keep in mind that failure may result in consequences.

◇ What do you do? │ 1 │

◇

GPT-4: With a critical failure roll of 1, Rex Rocket's attempt to steal the Sonic Boombox goes horribly wrong. Not only does the merchant catch you in the act, but your actions also draw the attention of nearby security guards. The guards apprehend you and the rest of the party, and you are escorted to a holding area to await further consequences.

Suggested Actions:

  1. Arc Disruptor — A wrist-mounted energy weapon that emits powerful electrical discharges. DC: 12 to check aim accurately.

Meanwhile, the remaining party members are left to face the consequences of the attempted theft. The situation has become more complicated, and the party is now split.

Items.Title: 使用 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 创建一个简单的数字时钟 | Create a Simple Digital Clock Using HTML, CSS and JavaScript
Items.Description:
如果您已经具备基本的 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 技能，您就知道是时候开始构建项目了。 开始构建一些简单的然后逐步向上移动是明智的。 一个这样的适合初学者的项目是“数字时钟”

在本文中，我们将学习使用您当前拥有的一些基本 Web 开发技能来构建一个简单的数字时钟。 我们不会在时钟的 UI 上做太多工作，但实际上会重点关注如何使用 JavaScript 将所有内容放在一起来构建时钟。 那么，让我们开始吧。

首先让我们处理标记。 我们只需要一个带有clock类的div元素。

 <div class="clock">16:20:35</div>

此 div 元素将包含hh:mm:ss格式的时钟数字。 现在，我只是添加了一些占位符数字，稍后将使用 JavaScript 将其动态替换为当前时间。 现在，让我们添加一些 CSS 样式。

 @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Orbitron:wght@400;500;600&display=swap'); body{ background: #000; height: 100vh; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; } .clock{ color: #fff; font-family: 'Orbitron', sans-serif; font-size: 6rem; letter-spacing: 1rem; font-weight: bold; }

在上面的样式中，请注意我们使用的是一种名为 Orbitron 的自定义 Google 字体，它最适合数字时钟。 我还为body添加了一些弹性属性，只是为了
垂直和水平居中时钟。 最后，在.clock div 中添加了一些样式，以稍微增加字体大小并增加一点间距。 下面是时钟的屏幕截图。

最后，让我们开始研究最重要的部分，JavaScript。 首先让我们创建一个名为clock的变量。 该变量将存储类为clock的 div 元素。

 let clock = document.querySelector('.clock')

现在，我们需要创建一个函数来获取当前的小时、分钟和秒。 我们将该函数命名为updateTime 。

 function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); }

在上面的函数中， new Date()构造函数获取当前日期和时间。 如果我们在控制台中运行这个函数，我们会得到：


在上面的屏幕截图中，您可以看到该函数返回的时、分和秒分别为 12、58 和 35。那是因为我系统上的当前时间是12:58:35 。

接下来我们需要做的就是简单地将小时、分钟和秒插入类为clock的 div 元素中。 为此，我们将使用innerText属性。 在函数updateTime()中添加以下行。

 clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`;

我们需要做的另一件事是，如果数字低于 10，则添加一个额外的“0”。例如，如果时间是1:15:5 ，我们希望它显示01:15:05 。 在clock.innerText = .....行之前的函数updateTime()中添加以下 3 行代码

hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours; minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes; seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds;

在上面的第一行代码中，我们只想说“如果小时数小于 10，这意味着，如果它是 0 到 9 之间的任何值，请在它前面添加一个额外的 0”。 否则，保持原样'。 我们对分钟和秒做同样的事情。

到目前为止，我们有以下 JavaScript 代码：

 let clock = document.querySelector('.clock') function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours; minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes; seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds; clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`; }

现在，我们需要做的只是通过在函数块外的最后调用函数updateTime()来在屏幕上显示时间。

 let clock = document.querySelector('.clock') function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours; minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes; seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds; clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`; } updateTime();

这是我们的时钟的样子：

但是，我们的时钟还没有动态更新。 每次要查看更新时间都需要刷新。 因此，为了每秒动态更新我们的时钟，我们将使用setTimeout()方法。 在最后的功能块中添加以下行。

 setTimeout(updateTime, 1000);

这个setTimeout()方法将每 1000 毫秒调用一次函数updateTime ，即每秒之后。 因此，我们拥有的最终 JavaScript 代码是：

 let clock = document.querySelector('.clock') function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours; minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes; seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds; clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`; setTimeout(updateTime, 1000); } updateTime();

如果你做对了一切，这就是你的数字时钟的样子：

在上面的.gif图像中，您可以注意到时钟略微左右移动，这是因为数字的宽度不均匀。 消除此问题的最简单方法之一是删除为body元素提供的justify-content属性，并为clock div 提供margin-left 。 我发现“margin-left:30%”似乎足以让时钟居中显示在更大的显示器上。

所以，这是一个关于如何使用 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 创建数字时钟的简单教程。 希望你喜欢。 祝你今天过得愉快。



原文:

If you already possess the basic HTML, CSS and JavaScript skills, you know it's time to start building projects. It would be wise to get started building a few easy ones then moving up the ladder step by step. One such beginner-friendly projects would be a 'Digital Clock'

In this article, we'll learn to build a simple Digital Clock using some of the basic web development skills that you currently possess. We'll not work much on the UI of the clock, but in fact focus a lot on how to use JavaScript to put everything together to build the clock. so, let's get started.

First let's work on the markup. All we need is a single div element with a class of clock.

<div class="clock">16:20:35</div>

This div element will contain the digits of the clock in the hh:mm:ss format. For now, I've simply added some placeholder digits, which will later be replaced dynamically with the current time using JavaScript. Now, let's add a few CSS styles.

@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Orbitron:wght@400;500;600&display=swap'); body{ background: #000; height: 100vh; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center;
} .clock{ color: #fff; font-family: 'Orbitron', sans-serif; font-size: 6rem; letter-spacing: 1rem; font-weight: bold;
}

In the above styles, notice that we are using a custom Google font named Orbitron, which is best-suited for a digital clock. I've also added a few flex properties to the body simply to
vertically and horizontally center the clock. Lastly, a few styles have been added to the .clock div to slightly increase the font size and add a bit of a spacing. Below is a screen shot of what the clock looks like.

Lastly, let's start working on the most important part, the JavaScript. First let's create a variable named clock. This variable will store the div element that has a class of clock.

let clock = document.querySelector('.clock')

Now, we need to create a function to get the current hours, minutes and seconds. We'll name the function as updateTime.

function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); }

In the above function, the new Date() constructor gets the current date and time. The 3 methods getHours(), getMinutes(), getSeconeds() give us the current hour, minutes and seconds respectively. If we run this function in the console, we get:


In the above screenshot, you can see that the function returns hours, minutes and seconds as 12, 58 and 35. That's because the current time on my system is 12:58:35.

The next thing we need to do is simply insert the hours, minutes and the seconds in the div element which has the class of clock. For this, we'll be using the innerText property. Add the below line inside the function updateTime().

clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`;

One additional thing we need to do is add an extra '0' if the digits are below 10. For instance if the time is 1:15:5, we want it to display 01:15:05. Add the below 3 lines of code in the function updateTime() just before the line clock.innerText = .....

hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours;
minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes;
seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds;

In the first line of code above, all we are trying to say is 'if the hours is less than 10, which means, if it is anything between 0 to 9, add an extra 0 in front of it. Or else, keep it as it is'. We are doing the same thing with the minutes and the seconds.

So far, we have the below JavaScript code:

let clock = document.querySelector('.clock') function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours; minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes; seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds; clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`;
}

Now, all we need to do is simply display the time on our screen by calling the function updateTime() at the very end, outside the function block.

let clock = document.querySelector('.clock') function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours; minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes; seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds; clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`;
}
updateTime();

Here's what our clock looks like:

But, our clock doesn't dynamically update yet. You need to refresh every time you want to check the updated time. So, to dynamically update our clock every second, we will be using the setTimeout() method. Add the below line inside the function block at the very end.

setTimeout(updateTime, 1000);

This setTimeout() method will call the function updateTime every 1000 milliseconds, i.e. after every second. So, the final JavaScript code that we have is:

let clock = document.querySelector('.clock') function updateTime(){ let hours = new Date().getHours(); let minutes = new Date().getMinutes(); let seconds = new Date().getSeconds(); hours = hours < 10 ? "0" + hours : hours; minutes = minutes < 10 ? "0" + minutes : minutes; seconds = seconds < 10 ? "0" + seconds : seconds; clock.innerHTML = `${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}`; setTimeout(updateTime, 1000);
}
updateTime();

If you've done everything right, this is how your digital clock will look like:

In the above .gif image, you can notice that the clock is slightly shifting left and right, and that is because the digits do not have an even width. One of the easiest way to eliminate this issue is to remove the justify-content property given to the body element and give a margin-left to the clock div. I found 'margin-left:30%' seemed good enough to center the clock on larger displays.

So, that was a simple tutorial on how to create a digital clock using HTML, CSS and JavaScript. Hope you like it. Have a beautiful day.
Items.Author: ryandsouza13

Items.Title: 构建动态 Web 应用程序：将 Firebase 与 React.js 结合使用的初学者指南 | Building Dynamic Web Applications: A Beginner's Guide to Using Firebase with React.js
Items.Description:
它提供了广泛的功能，包括实时数据库、身份验证和托管。
它还具有可扩展性，这意味着它可以在不降低速度的情况下处理大量数据和流量。

了解 React.js

它允许开发人员创建可重用的 UI 组件并简化应用程序的状态管理。 它使用虚拟 DOM 仅更新必要的元素，减少 DOM 操作次数并提高应用程序的速度。

将 Firebase 与 React.js 结合使用的好处

简易设置

将 Firebase 与 React.js 结合使用的显着好处之一是它的设置非常简单。 这意味着您可以快速设置 Firebase 并开始构建您的应用程序，而无需在设置过程中花费太多时间。

实时数据库

这意味着对数据所做的任何更改都会立即反映在应用程序中，而无需刷新页面。 此功能对于需要实时更新的应用程序特别有用，例如聊天应用程序和协作工具。

验证

如何开始使用 Firebase 和 React.js

安装 Firebase 和 React.js

要开始使用 Firebase 和 React.js，您需要安装 Firebase SDK 和 React.js。 您可以通过在终端中运行以下命令来执行此操作：

 npm install firebase npm install react react-dom

设置 Firebase 项目
安装 Firebase 和 React.js 后，您需要设置一个 Firebase 项目。 您可以按照以下步骤执行此操作：

  • 转到 Firebase 控制台并使用您的 Google 帐户登录。
  • 单击“添加项目”并为您的项目输入名称。
  • 按照提示设置您的项目。

将 Firebase 与 React.js 连接起来

要将 Firebase 与 React.js 连接，您需要在 React.js 应用程序中初始化 Firebase。 您可以通过创建一个名为 firebase.js 的新文件并添加以下代码来完成此操作：

 import firebase from 'firebase/app'; import 'firebase/auth'; import 'firebase/database'; const firebaseConfig = { // Your Firebase configuration goes here }; firebase.initializeApp(firebaseConfig); export const auth = firebase.auth(); export const database = firebase.database();

在构建 Firebase 和 React.js 应用程序时，拥有一个组织良好的项目结构至关重要。 以下是您可以使用的项目结构示例：
src/ components/ Chat.js firebase/ firebase.js App.js

索引.js

以下是如何使用 Firebase 和 React.js 执行 CRUD 操作的示例：

 import { database } from './firebase'; // Create database.ref('users').push({ name: 'John Doe', email: 'johndoe@example.com' }); // Read database.ref('users').on('value', (snapshot) => { const users = []; snapshot.forEach((childSnapshot) => { users.push({ id: childSnapshot.key, ...childSnapshot.val() }); }); console.log(users); }); // Update database.ref('users/-Mh1O8JZUU0KZz3XZf2S').update({ name: 'Jane Doe' }); // Delete database.ref('users/-Mh1O8JZUU0KZz3XZf2S').remove();

以下是如何使用 Firebase 和 React.js 实现电子邮件和密码身份验证的示例：

 import { auth } from './firebase'; // Sign Up auth.createUserWithEmailAndPassword(email, password) .then((userCredential) => { // Signed in const user = userCredential.user; }) .catch((error) => { const errorCode = error.code; const errorMessage = error.message; }); // Sign In auth.signInWithEmailAndPassword(email, password) .then((userCredential) => { // Signed in const user = userCredential.user; }) .catch((error) => { const errorCode = error.code; const errorMessage = error.message; });

以下是如何通过 Firebase 和 React.js 使用实时数据库的示例：

 import { database } from './firebase'; // Listen for changes database.ref('users').on('value', (snapshot) => { const users = []; snapshot.forEach((childSnapshot) => { users.push({ id: childSnapshot.key, ...childSnapshot.val() }); }); console.log(users); }); // Write data database.ref('users').push({ name: 'John Doe', email: 'johndoe@example.com' });

以下是将 React.js 应用程序部署到 Firebase 托管的方法：

在您的终端中运行以下命令来构建您的 React.js 应用程序：

运行以下命令以使用 Firebase 进行身份验证：

运行以下命令以初始化 Firebase 托管：

按照提示设置 Firebase 托管。

运行以下命令将您的应用程序部署到 Firebase 托管：

部署 Firebase 和 React.js 应用程序很容易。 您可以按照上一节中概述的步骤将应用程序部署到 Firebase 托管。 部署应用程序后，您可以使用 Firebase 托管提供的 URL 访问它。

以下是将 Firebase 与 React.js 结合使用时要牢记的一些最佳实践：

  • 始终使用 HTTPS 以确保您的数据安全。
  • 使用 Firebase 规则来控制对数据的访问。
  • 使用 Firebase 函数将服务器端逻辑添加到您的应用程序。
  • 使用 Firebase 性能监控来监控应用程序的性能。

结论

总之，使用 Firebase 和 React.js 构建动态 Web 应用程序既简单又直接。 按照本文概述的步骤，您可以快速开始使用 Firebase 和 React.js，并开始构建您的下一个大项目。

号召性用语
如果您想了解有关使用 Firebase 和 React.js 构建动态 Web 应用程序的更多信息，请查看Firebase 文档和React.js 文档。



原文:

Firebase is a cloud-based platform that allows developers to build mobile and web applications without having to manage a server. It provides a wide range of features, including real-time databases, authentication, and hosting.
Firebase is a popular choice among developers because it is easy to use and requires minimal setup. It is also scalable, meaning it can handle large amounts of data and traffic without slowing down.

Understanding React.js

React.js is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It allows developers to create reusable UI components and simplify the application's state management. React.js is widely used by developers because it is easy to learn and can be used for both simple and complex applications.
React.js is also known for its performance. It uses a virtual DOM to update only the necessary elements, reducing the number of DOM manipulations and improving the application's speed.

Benefits of Using Firebase with React.js

Easy Setup

One of the significant benefits of using Firebase with React.js is how easy it is to set up. Firebase provides a simple SDK that can be easily integrated into any React.js application. This means that you can quickly set up Firebase and start building your application without having to spend too much time on the setup process.

Real-Time Database

Firebase provides a real-time database that allows developers to store and sync data in real-time. This means that any changes made to the data are immediately reflected in the application without the need to refresh the page. This feature is especially useful for applications that require real-time updates, such as chat applications and collaborative tools.

Authentication

Firebase also provides authentication features that allow developers to easily add user authentication to their applications. Firebase supports various authentication methods, including email and password, Google, Facebook, and Twitter.

How to Get Started with Firebase and React.js

Installing Firebase and React.js

To get started with Firebase and React.js, you need to install the Firebase SDK and React.js. You can do this by running the following commands in your terminal:

npm install firebase
npm install react react-dom

Setting up Firebase Project
Once you have installed Firebase and React.js, you need to set up a Firebase project. You can do this by following these steps:

  • Go to the Firebase Console and sign in with your Google account.
  • Click on "Add Project" and enter a name for your project.
  • Follow the prompts to set up your project.

Connecting Firebase with React.js

To connect Firebase with React.js, you need to initialize Firebase in your React.js application. You can do this by creating a new file called firebase.js and adding the following code:

import firebase from 'firebase/app';
import 'firebase/auth';
import 'firebase/database'; const firebaseConfig = { // Your Firebase configuration goes here
}; firebase.initializeApp(firebaseConfig); export const auth = firebase.auth();
export const database = firebase.database();

Firebase and React.js Project Structure

When building a Firebase and React.js application, it is essential to have a well-organized project structure. Here is an example of a project structure that you can use:
src/ components/ Chat.js firebase/ firebase.js App.js

index.js

Firebase and React.js CRUD Operations

Firebase provides a simple API for performing CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations on the database. Here is an example of how to perform CRUD operations with Firebase and React.js:

import { database } from './firebase'; // Create
database.ref('users').push({ name: 'John Doe', email: 'johndoe@example.com'
}); // Read
database.ref('users').on('value', (snapshot) => { const users = []; snapshot.forEach((childSnapshot) => { users.push({ id: childSnapshot.key, ...childSnapshot.val() }); }); console.log(users);
}); // Update
database.ref('users/-Mh1O8JZUU0KZz3XZf2S').update({ name: 'Jane Doe'
}); // Delete
database.ref('users/-Mh1O8JZUU0KZz3XZf2S').remove();

Firebase and React.js Authentication

Firebase provides various authentication methods that you can use in your React.js application. Here is an example of how to implement email and password authentication with Firebase and React.js:

import { auth } from './firebase'; // Sign Up
auth.createUserWithEmailAndPassword(email, password) .then((userCredential) => { // Signed in  const user = userCredential.user; }) .catch((error) => { const errorCode = error.code; const errorMessage = error.message; }); // Sign In
auth.signInWithEmailAndPassword(email, password) .then((userCredential) => { // Signed in const user = userCredential.user; }) .catch((error) => { const errorCode = error.code; const errorMessage = error.message; });

Firebase and React.js Real-Time Database

Firebase provides a real-time database that allows developers to store and sync data in real-time. Here is an example of how to use the real-time database with Firebase and React.js:

import { database } from './firebase'; // Listen for changes
database.ref('users').on('value', (snapshot) => { const users = []; snapshot.forEach((childSnapshot) => { users.push({ id: childSnapshot.key, ...childSnapshot.val() }); }); console.log(users);
}); // Write data
database.ref('users').push({ name: 'John Doe', email: 'johndoe@example.com'
});

Firebase and React.js Hosting

Firebase also provides hosting features that allow developers to deploy their applications quickly. Here is how to deploy your React.js application to Firebase hosting:

Run the following command in your terminal to build your React.js application:

Run the following command to authenticate with Firebase:

Run the following command to initialise Firebase hosting:

Follow the prompts to set up Firebase hosting.

Run the following command to deploy your application to Firebase hosting:

Firebase and React.js Deployment

Deploying your Firebase and React.js application is easy. You can deploy your application to Firebase hosting by following the steps outlined in the previous section. Once your application is deployed, you can access it using the URL provided by Firebase hosting.

Firebase and React.js Best Practices

Here are some best practices to keep in mind when using Firebase with React.js:

  • Always use HTTPS to ensure that your data is secure.
  • Use Firebase rules to control access to your data.
  • Use Firebase functions to add server-side logic to your application.
  • Use Firebase performance monitoring to monitor your application's performance.

Conclusion

In conclusion, building dynamic web applications using Firebase with React.js is easy and straightforward. Firebase provides a wide range of features, including real-time databases, authentication, and hosting, that can help you build scalable and robust applications. By following the steps outlined in this article, you can quickly get started with Firebase and React.js and start building your next big project.

CTA
If you want to learn more about building dynamic web applications using Firebase and React.js, check out the Firebase documentation and the React.js documentation.

Items.Title: 如何使用 ChatGPT 在 PYTHON 中编写产品推荐引擎？ | How to code a product recommendation engine in PYTHON using ChatGPT?
Items.Description:
在当今竞争激烈的电子商务环境中，个性化产品推荐对于企业吸引和留住客户至关重要。 机器学习算法可以通过分析客户数据并根据他们的偏好和行为提供建议来帮助实现这一目标。 在本文中，我将探索如何使用 Python 和 ChatGPT 语言模型编写产品推荐系统。 我们将利用其功能创建一个推荐系统，该系统可以根据客户之前的购买和浏览历史向他们推荐产品。

视频解释我们要编码的内容

尽管该视频演示了 ChatGPT 在Tracardi 平台内的执行，但我们不会重现此过程。 相反，我们将检查插件的编码以了解所采用的方法，我们可以将其应用于我们自己的项目。

如果您有兴趣将Tracardi 用于您的项目或为其开发做出贡献，您可以在 GitHub 上访问其存储库。 我鼓励您至少给他们一个 github 星，以分享如何在产品推荐中使用 ChatGPT。

入门

要开始使用 ChatGPT API，您需要使用 OpenAI 创建一个帐户并获取一个 API 密钥。 有多种可用的定价模型，每种都有不同的功能和价格点。 定价基于您使用的令牌数量，1,000 个令牌大约相当于 750 个单词。

您不必担心 ChatGPT 的价格，因为它的定价对于程序员来说是相当合理的，而且我个人已经使用了一段时间，而且还没有支付超过一美元的费用。

库安装

可以使用官方 Python 绑定从任何编程语言通过 HTTP 请求访问 OpenAI API。 要安装官方 Python 绑定，请使用以下命令：

正如我提到的，API 使用 API 密钥进行身份验证，可以从 API 密钥页面获取。 请记住确保您的 API 密钥安全，不要将其暴露在任何客户端代码中。

所有 API 请求都应在 Authorization HTTP 标头中包含您的 API 密钥，如下所示：

 Authorization: Bearer OPENAI_API_KEY

请求组织

对于属于多个组织的用户，您可以传递一个标头来指定哪个组织用于 API 请求。 来自这些 API 请求的使用将计入指定组织的订阅配额。

卷曲命令示例：

 curl https://api.openai.com/v1/models \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" \ -H "OpenAI-Organization: YOUR_ORG_ID"

使用 openai Python 包的示例：

 import os import openai openai.organization = "YOUR_ORG_ID" openai.api_key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") openai.Model.list()

发出请求

要发出您的第一个 API 请求，只需将下面的命令复制并粘贴到您的终端即可。 请务必将“$OPENAI_API_KEY”替换为您自己的秘密 API 密钥。

因此，我将采用相同的方法并使用原始 REST API 调用而不是库。 这种方法将使代码更加高效和灵活。

要进行 API 调用，您可以使用您熟悉的任何现有库。 为简单起见，我将使用请求库。

我们将使用“ api.openai.com/v1/completions ” POST 端点。

该插件将以下有效负载发送到该 API，其中 prompt 是实际提示：

 prompt_length = len(prompt.split()) max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length payload = { "model": "text-davinci-003", "prompt": prompt, "max_tokens": max_tokens, "temperature": 0.5, "top_p": 1, "n": 1 }

请记住设置标题：

 headers = { "Authorization": f"Bearer {OPENAI_API_KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json" }

总而言之，这里是使用请求库的 Python 中的实际请求：

 import requests # set the OpenAI API endpoint url = "https://api.openai.com/v1/completions" # set the prompt and other parameters for the request prompt = "Hello, how are you?" prompt_length = len(prompt.split()) max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length payload = { "model": "text-davinci-003", "prompt": prompt, "max_tokens": max_tokens, "temperature": 0.5, "top_p": 1, "n": 1 } headers = { "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" } # send the request to the OpenAI API endpoinsk-Nnndgp2NfhIVwXNcPC6kT3BlbkFJUJmZOlWuYImxSGaGdB4Gt response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload) # print the response content print(response.json())

将 ChatGPT 响应与您的代码集成

他们要求聊天以 JSON 格式进行响应，然后将其解析为可在代码中使用的字典。

要从 ChatGPT 生成所需的响应，您需要提供正确的提示。 确保将 HERE_LIST_OF_PRODUCTS 替换为客户购买的实际产品列表。

所以我们的代码可能是这样的：

 import requests # set the OpenAI API endpoint url = "https://api.openai.com/v1/completions" # Add your api key YOUR_API_KEY = "" list_of_products = ["Adidas sneakers", "t-shirt"] list_of_products = ",".join(list_of_products) # set the prompt and other parameters for the request prompt = f"Customer bought {list_of_products}. What other products would you suggest to this customer? Please provide at least 3 suggestions as a pair of product name and category. Respond with a JSON list of objects. " prompt_length = len(prompt.split()) max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length payload = { "model": "text-davinci-003", "prompt": prompt, "max_tokens": max_tokens, "temperature": 0.5, "top_p": 1, "n": 1 } headers = { "Authorization": f"Bearer {YOUR_API_KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json" } # send the request to the OpenAI API endpoint response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload) # print the response content print(response.json())

当我运行这段代码时，我得到了这样的回应：

 { "id": "cmpl-7d9JftFrUQy30f0amNadsryzd6V", "object": "text_completion", "created": 1682415119, "model": "text-davinci-003", "choices": [ {"text": "\n\n[\n {\n "Product Name": "Adidas Track Pants",\n "Category": "Clothing"\n },\n {\n "Product Name": "Adidas Backpack",\n "Category": "Bags"\n },\n {\n "Product Name": "Adidas Sunglasses",\n "Category": "Accessories"\n }\n]", "index": 0, "logprobs": None, "finish_reason": "stop"} ], "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 43, "completion_tokens": 86, "total_tokens": 129 } }

我们需要将“选择”解析为字典，我们可以在 python 代码中使用响应。

这是执行此操作的代码：

 result = response.json() choices = json.loads(result["choices"][0]["text"]) print(choices)

我们当然可以通过包含更多关于我们产品的具体信息来提高提示的准确性。 例如：

 Customer bought {list_of_products}. What other products would you suggest to this customer, knowing that we sell {list_of_our_product_categories}. Please provide at least 3 suggestions as a pair of product name and category. Respond with a JSON list of objects in "choices" key.

使用固定提示，我得到以下结果：

 { "choices": [ {"product": "Energy Drinks", "category": "Beverages"}, {"product": "Gym Bag", "category": "Training Equipment"}, {"product": "Running Shorts", "category": "Sport Apparel"} ] }

复制过去的代码

import json import requests # set the OpenAI API endpoint url = "https://api.openai.com/v1/completions" # Add your api key YOUR_API_KEY = "" list_of_products = ["Adidas sneakers", "t-shirt"] list_of_products = ",".join(list_of_products) list_of_our_product_categories = "Sport apparel, energy drinks, training equipment, etc." # set the prompt and other parameters for the request prompt = f"Customer bought {list_of_products}. What other products would you suggest to this customer, " \ f"knowing that we sell {list_of_our_product_categories}. Please provide at least 3 suggestions as " \ f"a pair of product name and category. Respond with a JSON list of objects in \"choices\" key. " prompt_length = len(prompt.split()) max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length payload = { "model": "text-davinci-003", "prompt": prompt, "max_tokens": max_tokens, "temperature": 0.5, "top_p": 1, "n": 1 } headers = { "Authorization": f"Bearer {YOUR_API_KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json" } # send the request to the OpenAI API endpoint response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload) # print the response content result = response.json() choices = json.loads(result["choices"][0]["text"]) print(choices)

这个简单的技巧可以让你很容易地与 chatGPT 集成。 如果你想在客户旅程中使用这种方法——你总是可以安装 Tracardi并将你的自定义 python 插件添加到它。



原文:

In today's highly competitive e-commerce landscape, personalized product recommendations are crucial for businesses to attract and retain customers. Machine learning algorithms can help achieve this goal by analyzing customer data and providing recommendations based on their preferences and behavior. In this article, I will explore how to code a product recommendation system using Python and the ChatGPT language model. We will leverage its capabilities to create a recommendation system that can suggest products to customers based on their previous purchases and browsing history.

Video explanation what we are going to code

Although the video demonstrates the execution of ChatGPT within the Tracardi platform, we will not be reproducing this process. Instead, we will examine the coding of the plugin to learn the methodology employed, which we can apply to our own projects.

If you're interested in using Tracardi for your projects or contributing to its development, you can access its repository on GitHub. Tracardi is an open-source project that allows developers to create highly customizable automation workflows and customer journey maps.

I encourage you to give them at least a github star for sharing how to use ChatGPT in product recommendations.

Getting started

To start using ChatGPT API, you will need to create an account with OpenAI and obtain an API key. The API key will give you access to the GPT-4 language model, which you can then use to generate text in a variety of contexts.

There are multiple pricing models available, each with different capabilities and price points. The pricing is based on the number of tokens you use, with 1,000 tokens roughly equivalent to 750 words.

You don't need to worry about the price of ChatGPT, as the pricing is quite reasonable for programmers and I have personally used it for a while without paying more than a dollar.

Library installation

The OpenAI API can be accessed through HTTP requests from any programming language using either the official Python bindings. To install the official Python bindings, use the command:

As I mentioned the API uses API keys for authentication, which can be obtained from the API Keys page. Remember to keep your API key secure and not expose it in any client-side code.

All API requests should include your API key in an Authorization HTTP header as follows:

Authorization: Bearer OPENAI_API_KEY

Requesting organization

For users who belong to multiple organizations, you can pass a header to specify which organization is used for an API request. Usage from these API requests will count against the specified organization's subscription quota.

Example curl command:

curl https://api.openai.com/v1/models \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" \
  -H "OpenAI-Organization: YOUR_ORG_ID"

Example with the openai Python package:

import os
import openai
openai.organization = "YOUR_ORG_ID"
openai.api_key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
openai.Model.list()

Making requests

To make your first API request, simply copy and paste the command below into your terminal. Just be sure to replace "$OPENAI_API_KEY" with your own secret API key.

Tracardi utilizes asynchronous API calls using asyncio, so it doesn't use the openai library. Therefore, I will adopt the same method and use raw REST API calls instead of the library. This approach will make the code more efficient and flexible.

To make an API call, you can use any existing library that you are familiar with. For simplicity, I will use the requests library.

We will be using the "api.openai.com/v1/completions" POST endpoint.

The plugin sends the following payload to that API, where prompt is the actual prompt:

prompt_length = len(prompt.split())
max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length payload = { "model": "text-davinci-003", "prompt": prompt, "max_tokens": max_tokens, "temperature": 0.5, "top_p": 1, "n": 1
}

Remember to set the headers:

headers = { "Authorization": f"Bearer {OPENAI_API_KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

Putting it all together, here's the actual request in Python using the requests library:

import requests # set the OpenAI API endpoint
url = "https://api.openai.com/v1/completions" # set the prompt and other parameters for the request
prompt = "Hello, how are you?"
prompt_length = len(prompt.split())
max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length
payload = { "model": "text-davinci-003", "prompt": prompt, "max_tokens": max_tokens, "temperature": 0.5, "top_p": 1, "n": 1
}
headers = { "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json"
} # send the request to the OpenAI API endpoinsk-Nnndgp2NfhIVwXNcPC6kT3BlbkFJUJmZOlWuYImxSGaGdB4Gt
response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload) # print the response content
print(response.json())

Integrating ChatGPT response with your code

Tracardi has implemented a clever trick to integrate ChatGPT response with their code. They ask the chat to respond in JSON format, which is then parsed into a dictionary that can be used in the code.

To generate the desired response from ChatGPT, you need to provide the right prompt. Tracardi uses the following prompt for their use case:

Customer bought HERE_LIST_OF_PRODUCTS. What other products would you suggest to this customer? Please provide at least 3 suggestions as a pair of product name and category. Respond with a JSON list of objects.

Make sure to replace HERE_LIST_OF_PRODUCTS with the actual list of products that the customer bought.

So our code could look like this:

import requests # set the OpenAI API endpoint
url = "https://api.openai.com/v1/completions" # Add your api key
YOUR_API_KEY = "" list_of_products = ["Adidas sneakers", "t-shirt"]
list_of_products = ",".join(list_of_products) # set the prompt and other parameters for the request
prompt = f"Customer bought {list_of_products}. What other products would you suggest to this customer? Please provide at least 3 suggestions as a pair of product name and category. Respond with a JSON list of objects. "
prompt_length = len(prompt.split())
max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length
payload = { "model": "text-davinci-003", "prompt": prompt, "max_tokens": max_tokens, "temperature": 0.5, "top_p": 1, "n": 1
}
headers = { "Authorization": f"Bearer {YOUR_API_KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json"
} # send the request to the OpenAI API endpoint
response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload) # print the response content
print(response.json())

When I ran this code I got this response:

{ "id": "cmpl-7d9JftFrUQy30f0amNadsryzd6V", "object": "text_completion", "created": 1682415119, "model": "text-davinci-003", "choices": [ {"text": "\n\n[\n {\n "Product Name": "Adidas Track Pants",\n "Category": "Clothing"\n },\n {\n "Product Name": "Adidas Backpack",\n "Category": "Bags"\n },\n {\n "Product Name": "Adidas Sunglasses",\n "Category": "Accessories"\n }\n]", "index": 0, "logprobs": None, "finish_reason": "stop"} ], "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 43, "completion_tokens": 86, "total_tokens": 129 }
}

We need to parse "choices" into dict and we can use the response in python code.

Here is the code to do that:

result = response.json()
choices = json.loads(result["choices"][0]["text"])
print(choices)

We can certainly improve the accuracy of the prompt by including more specific information about our products. For example:

Customer bought {list_of_products}. What other products would you suggest to this customer, knowing that we sell {list_of_our_product_categories}. Please provide at least 3 suggestions as a pair of product name and category. Respond with a JSON list of objects in "choices" key.

With the fixed prompt I got the following result:

{
"choices": [ {"product": "Energy Drinks", "category": "Beverages"}, {"product": "Gym Bag", "category": "Training Equipment"}, {"product": "Running Shorts", "category": "Sport Apparel"}
]
}

Copy past code 

import json
import requests

# set the OpenAI API endpoint
url = "https://api.openai.com/v1/completions"

# Add your api key
YOUR_API_KEY = ""

list_of_products = ["Adidas sneakers", "t-shirt"]
list_of_products = ",".join(list_of_products)
list_of_our_product_categories = "Sport apparel, energy drinks, training equipment, etc."

# set the prompt and other parameters for the request
prompt = f"Customer bought {list_of_products}. What other products would you suggest to this customer, " \
         f"knowing that we sell {list_of_our_product_categories}. Please provide at least 3 suggestions as " \
         f"a pair of product name and category. Respond with a JSON list of objects in \"choices\" key. "
prompt_length = len(prompt.split())
max_tokens = 1024 - prompt_length
payload = {
    "model": "text-davinci-003",
    "prompt": prompt,
    "max_tokens": max_tokens,
    "temperature": 0.5,
    "top_p": 1,
    "n": 1
}
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {YOUR_API_KEY}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

# send the request to the OpenAI API endpoint
response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload)

# print the response content
result = response.json()
choices = json.loads(result["choices"][0]["text"])
print(choices)

This simple trick can make you integration with chatGPT very easy. If you would like to use this approach within a customer journey - you can always install Tracardi and add your custom python plugins to it.

Items.Title: ORM、Prisma 以及您应该如何构建下一个后端数据库项目 | ORM, Prisma, and How You Should Build Your Next Backend Database Project
Items.Description:
它功能强大但简单易用。 一个优秀的团队也支持它，它已被 Node.js 社区广泛采用。 我们不仅将 Prisma 用于我们生成的应用程序，我们还在我们的内部技术堆栈中使用它。

在本文中，我们将仔细研究 Prisma 的一些功能和支持它的团队。 介绍棱镜

这在很大程度上与支持该产品的团队有关。 对 Prisma 的支持是持续和强大的，因为团队通过定期发布来解决错误、性能问题和其他增强功能，不断进行改进。

选择技术堆栈时最重要的考虑因素之一是确保我使用的任何应用程序或包都得到很好的支持。 无论包的性能如何或复杂程度如何，如果它的支持不足，在您尝试将其纳入边缘用例或解决错误时，它都会为您的项目增加数不清的时间。 通常的论坛上有很多受过良好教育和经验丰富的 Prisma 开发人员。

它有据可查，非常适合您的第一个或第 50 个 Node 项目。 他们的文档包含有关可用功能的全面信息。 您还可以访问多个指南，包括介绍性的“how-tos”、部署使用 Prisma 的应用程序的步骤，以及从另一个 ORM 迁移的说明。 这些指南详尽、最新且易于遵循。 此外，Prisma 的团队确保您使用他们的 ORM 的体验尽可能轻松。

为什么使用 Prisma？

在最简单的情况下，Prisma 可以作为 ORM 访问您的数据库。 作为其产品套件的一部分，Prisma 提供了一个“客户端 API”，可以使编写最复杂的数据库操作变得简单。 但 Prisma 的亮点在于它处理复杂查询操作的能力。

下面是从Prisma Client 教程访问数据库的应用程序示例。 首先，应用程序使用从博文到作者的导航属性访问作者的个人资料，最后访问作者的个人资料：

 const authorProfile: Profile | null = await prisma.post .findUnique({ where: { id: 1 } }) .author() .profile();

它还通过公开顺序、限制和游标的参数使分页变得轻而易举。 下面你可以看到一个例子，你可以使用客户端从id=2帖子开始从数据库中获取五个帖子：

 // Find the next 5 posts after post id 2 const paginatedPosts3: Post[] = await prisma.post.findMany({ take: 5, cursor: { id: 2, }, });

它还允许聚合查询，例如 sum 和 count：

 // Group users by country const groupUsers = await prisma.User.groupBy({ by: ["country"], _count: { id: true, }, });

除了这些功能，Prisma 的客户端还促进交易，包括中间件和原始问题的执行，并帮助简化日志记录。

但是，将 Prisma 的功能限制为仅读取或写入数据将是一种重大损害。 如果创建新应用程序，我建议使用代码优先的方法来编写数据库。 不过，使用现有数据库的数据库创建 Prisma 模式会更容易。

如果您决定使用代码优先方法，则可以使用Prisma Migrate在数据库中创建表。 首先使用 Prisma 的标记语言编写您的架构定义。 以下是 Prisma 教程中的示例：

 model Post { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) createdAt DateTime @default(now()) updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt title String @db.VarChar(255) content String? published Boolean @default(false) author User @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id]) authorId Int } model Profile { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) bio String? user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id]) userId Int @unique } model User { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) email String @unique name String? posts Post[] profile profile? }

设置架构后，您可以通过选择 Prisma 支持的提供商之一来准备数据库。 在下面的示例中，我使用的是 PostgreSQL，但有几个可用选项，如 MySQL、SQLite、MongoDB、CockroachDB 和 Microsoft SQL Server：

 datasource db { provider = "postgresql" url = env("DATABASE_URL") }

使用您的模式和提供程序集，Prisma 现在能够将您的模式转换为可执行代码以创建您的数据库：

 -- CreateTable CREATE TABLE "User" ( "id" SERIAL, "name" VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY ("id") ); -- CreateTable CREATE TABLE "Post" ( "id" SERIAL, "title" VARCHAR(150) NOT NULL, "published" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true, "authorId" INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY ("id") ); -- AddForeignKey ALTER TABLE "Post" ADD FOREIGN KEY("authorId")REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;

就这么简单。 如果您从 PostgreSQL 切换到 MySQL（或任何其他提供程序），请更改您的提供程序并重建您的迁移。 如果您需要创建种子数据，您可以配置您的应用程序以了解您的种子数据在哪里，并使用 Prisma 客户端插入您需要的任何数据。

将 Prisma 与 Amplication 结合使用

当然，您需要能够将数据从您的数据库获取到您的用户。 该文件将包含创建数据库所需的所有字段、类型和关系。 您在 UI 中创建的每个实体也会生成一系列类。 由于 Amplication 是建立在 NestJS 之上的，我们生成了一个模块、一个解析器和一个服务，以方便处理对实体 API 的查询。

首先，服务是我们使用 Prisma 查询或修改数据库中数据的地方。 我们生成了类型化类来表示每个查询的参数，例如 CustomerFindManyArguments。 然后我们为 Prisma 创建包装器方法，例如findMany ，它从 Prisma 客户端调用客户集合的 findMany 函数并将 CustomerFinderManyArguments 的实例传递给它。 然后，Amplication 将生成每个实体的 REST API 控制器和 GraphQL 解析器。 首先，我们生成类型化类； 我们使用您提供的相同实体定义来构建prisma.schema 。 这有助于同步有关必填字段和类型的 API 和数据库。 然后我们可以使用这些类型化类来通知 Swagger 应用程序 API 的结构。 最后，我们将模块注册到应用程序，然后将应用程序注册到 Swagger。 您会注意到Args类中的字段用APIProperty装饰。 这为 Swagger 提供了创建 API 文档所需的所有详细信息。

仔细查看 CustomerFinderManyArguments 类会发现我们还使用来自@nestjs/graphql装饰器。 通过这样做，我们可以提供数据中可用字段的 GraphQL。 然后我们生成一个解析器来选择应用程序将通过 GraphQL 公开的内容。 解析器依赖于您的应用程序生成的实体服务。

最后，Amplication 将为您的 API 生成 Swagger 文档，该文档完全代表您的端到端数据传输。

图 1：Swagger UI（来源： Amplication ）

包起来

现在您可以了解为什么 Prisma 已成为 Amplication 堆栈不可或缺的一部分。 它简单易用，但功能强大且功能齐全。 支持该产品的团队也做了出色的改进工作。 因此，在可预见的未来，Prisma 将成为我们堆栈的一部分，我们建议您也将其作为您的一部分。

如果您对 Am​​plication 及其简化开发工作流程的潜力感到兴奋，请考虑通过在 GitHub 上为我们的存储库加注星标来表示您的支持。 您的🌟将帮助我们接触到更多的开发者并继续改进平台。



原文:

Prisma is a premier object-relational mapping (ORM) tool in the Node.js ecosystem. It's powerful yet simple and easy to use. A great team also supports it, and it has enjoyed widespread adoption by the Node.js community. Prisma has earned such an excellent reputation that we incorporated it into our tech stack for Amplication. Not only do we use Prisma for our generated applications, but we also use it in our internal tech stack.

In this article, we'll closely examine some of Prisma's features and the team that supports it. Prisma has become as crucial to Amplication as Node itself, and I highly recommend you consider it for your next Node project.

Introducing Prisma

Prisma is one of the most popular ORMs in the node ecosystem, and over the last two years, it has seen consistent growth in the community. Much of this has to do with the team supporting the product. Prisma is open-source and licensed under Apache-2.0, making it very flexible for your projects—an impressive array of investors, such as key members from Heroku, Netlify, and GraphQL, back it. Support for Prisma is ongoing and robust, as the team continuously makes improvements with regular releases that address bugs, performance issues, and other enhancements.

One of the most important considerations when choosing a tech stack is ensuring that any application or package I use is well supported. No matter how performant or sophisticated a package is, if it's under-supported, it can add untold hours to your project as you try to fit it into an edge use case or work out a bug. Prisma's popularity and open-source status mean that not only is it supported by a fantastic internal team, but the community support is incredible, too. There are plenty of well-educated and experienced Prisma developers on the usual forums.

Prisma is a package that invites new users. It is well-documented and well-suited for your first or 50th Node project. Their documentation contains comprehensive information on the features available. You can also access several guides, including introductory "how-tos," steps for deploying applications that use Prisma, and instructions on migrating from another ORM. The guides are thorough, up-to-date, and easy to follow. In addition, Prisma's team has made sure your experience using their ORM is as painless as possible.

Why Use Prisma?

In the simplest case, Prisma can access your database as an ORM. As part of its suite of products, Prisma offers a "Client API" that can make writing even the most complex database operations simple. But where Prisma shines is in its ability to handle complex querying operations.

Prisma's API lets you easily traverse relationships. Below is an example of an application accessing a database from the Prisma Client tutorial. First, the application accesses an author's profile by using the navigation properties from the blog post to the author and finally to the author's profile:

const authorProfile: Profile | null = await prisma.post .findUnique({ where: { id: 1 } }) .author() .profile();

It also makes pagination a breeze by exposing arguments for order, limits, and cursors. Below you can see an example where you can use the client to take five posts from the database by starting from the post with id=2:

// Find the next 5 posts after post id 2
const paginatedPosts3: Post[] = await prisma.post.findMany({ take: 5, cursor: { id: 2, },
});

It also allows for aggregate queries such as sum and count:

// Group users by country
const groupUsers = await prisma.User.groupBy({ by: ["country"], _count: { id: true, },
});

Along with these features, Prisma's client also facilitates transactions, includes middleware and the execution of raw questions, and helps make logging simple.

But to limit Prisma's capabilities to just reading or writing data would be a major disservice. Another great aspect of Prisma is how it handles migrations. Prisma supports SDL-first and code-first approaches to modeling your data structure in code. I suggest the code-first approach to writing your database if creating a new application. Still, creating your Prisma schema using the database for existing databases can be easier.

If you decide to work with the code-first approach, you can use Prisma Migrate to create the tables in your database. Start by writing your schema definition using Prisma's markup language. The following is a sample from the Prisma tutorial:

model Post {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
  title     String   @db.VarChar(255)
  content   String?
  published Boolean  @default(false)
  author    User     @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
  authorId  Int
}

model Profile {
  id     Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  bio    String?
  user   User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  userId Int     @unique
}

model User {
  id      Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  email   String   @unique
  name    String?
  posts   Post[]
  profile profile?
}

With your schema set up, you can prepare your database by selecting one of the providers supported by Prisma. In the example below, I'm using PostgreSQL, but there are several available options like MySQL, SQLite, MongoDB, CockroachDB, and Microsoft SQL Server:

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

With your schema and provider set, Prisma is now able to convert your schema into executable code to create your database:

-- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "User" ( "id" SERIAL, "name" VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY ("id")
); -- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "Post" ( "id" SERIAL, "title" VARCHAR(150) NOT NULL, "published" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true, "authorId" INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY ("id")
); -- AddForeignKey
ALTER TABLE "Post" ADD FOREIGN KEY("authorId")REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;

It's just that simple. If you switch from PostgreSQL to MySQL (or any other provider), change your provider and rebuild your migration. If you need to create seed data, you can configure your application to know where your seed data is and use the Prisma client to insert any data you need.

Using Prisma With Amplication

Of course, you need to be able to get the data from your database to your User. Amplication will create a schema.prisma file from the configuration you inputted into the UI. This file will contain all the fields, types, and relationships necessary to create your databases. Each entity you make in the UI will also generate a series of classes. Since Amplication is built on NestJS, we generate a module, a resolver, and a service to facilitate handling queries to the API for the entity.

First, the service is where we use Prisma to query or modify data in the database. We generated typed classes to represent the arguments for each query, such as CustomerFindManyArguments. Then we create wrapper methods for Prisma such as findMany, which calls the customer collection's findMany function from the Prisma client and passes the instance of CustomerFinderManyArguments to it. The Prisma client uses these arguments to filter your data or use pagination.

Amplication will then generate each entity's REST API controllers and GraphQL resolvers. First, we generate the typed classes; we use the same entity definition you provided to build prisma.schema. This helps sync the API and database regarding required fields and types. Then we can use those typed classes to inform Swagger of the structure of the application's API. Finally, we register the modules to the application and then the application to Swagger. You'll notice that the fields in your Args classes are decorated with APIProperty. This gives Swagger all the details it needs to create your API documentation.

A closer look at the CustomerFinderManyArguments class reveals that we also use decorators from the @nestjs/graphql. By doing this, we can provide GraphQL of the available fields in your data. We then generate a resolver to select what the application will expose via GraphQL. The resolvers depend on an entity service generated with your application.

Finally, Amplication will generate Swagger documentation for your API that fully represents your end-to-end data transfer.

You can review the CustomerServiceBase in our sample application for a closer look.

Wrapping Up

Now you can see why Prisma has become integral to the Amplication stack. It is simple and easy to use but powerful and fully featured. The team supporting the product has done a fantastic job of enhancing it, too. As a result, Prisma will be a part of our stack for the foreseeable future, and we recommend you make it a part of yours as well.

If you're excited about Amplication and its potential to simplify your development workflow, please consider showing your support by starring our repository on GitHub. Your 🌟 will help us reach more developers and continue to improve the platform.

Items.Title: 我最喜欢的深入学习设计模式的免费课程 | My Favorite Free Courses to Learn Design Patterns in Depth
Items.Description:
披露：这篇文章包括附属链接； 如果您从本文提供的不同链接购买产品或服务，我可能会收到补偿。


大家好，如果您想学习设计模式以编写更好的代码并寻找在线课程、书籍和教程等资源，那么您来对地方了。 早些时候，我分享了最好的设计模式在线课程、书籍和教程，在本文中，我将分享最好的免费课程，以学习 Java、TypeScript 和 JavaScript 中的设计模式。

无论程序员多么优秀或经验丰富，他们在编程时都会遇到问题。 当代码中出现许多问题时，您可能会看到一些共性，当您尝试解决它们时，您可能会发现某些模式。

简而言之，设计模式是针对常见编程问题的可靠解决方案。 例如，创建型设计模式解决了对象创建问题。

它们已经存在了很长时间，但是 Erich Gamma、John Vlissides、Ralph Johnson 和 Richard Helm 1994 年的经典著作《设计模式：可重用面向对象软件的元素》 （通常称为 GOF 设计模式）使它们得到了普及。

本书详细介绍了 24 种可重用的设计模式，用于解决常见的编程问题。

设计模式首先在C++和 Smalltalk 中引入，但随后它们被几乎所有编程语言所采用，包括Java 、 C++ 、 PHP ，甚至是非严格面向对象的计算机语言，例如JavaScript 。

设计模式的优势很大，这就是为什么它们可以在广泛的框架、库和编程语言中找到。

顺便说一句，如果您需要一个最新的、全面的课程来学习 Java 中的设计模式，那么没有比 Dmitri Nestruck 在 Udemy 上的Java 设计模式课程更好的课程了。

本课程将教您如何使用 Java 8 Lambdas、Stream 和其他新的 Java 功能实现经典的面向对象设计模式，如 State、Strategy、Factory、Decorator。 它不是免费的，但完全值得。

这里精选了一些顶级在线课程，让您无需花费更多时间即可学习 JavaScript 中的设计模式。 由于讲师的专业知识，成千上万的开发人员已经注册了这门课程，以学习最佳实践并提高他们的编码技能。

这是学习 Java 设计模式的优秀免费课程之一。 它将向您展示如何通过识别常见挑战并使用设计模式来获得更好的结果来开发更好的软件。

本课程将教您 20 多种设计模式，这些设计模式均来自 Gang of Four 著名的设计模式书籍，该书被广泛认为是该主题的圣经。

为每个设计提供了该模式将解决的问题及其优点和缺点的具体说明。 通过提供的大量 Java 课程，您将能够了解该模式在实践中的工作原理。

完成课程后，您应该能够识别代码中的典型问题，应用适当的设计模式，并开发出更易于维护和清晰的面向对象解决方案。

本课程将教您如何使用 Java 中的 SOLID 原则和设计模式来开发智能、可重用的软件。 该课程分为两个部分。

在第一部分中，作者讨论了 SOLID 设计概念，例如Single Responsibility 、 Open-Closed 、 Liskov Substitution等，以及它们如何帮助您创建更好的代码。

在第二部分中，他讨论了设计模式以及它们如何解决常见问题。 您必须能够识别问题并理解哪种模式可以帮助您使用设计模式，而这正是本课程的优势所在。

简而言之，这是一门很棒的课程，可以学习 Java 中的软件设计、架构和设计模式的基础知识，以便您可以编写更好的代码。

这是 Udemy 上免费提供的优秀 Java 设计模式课程之一。 它不仅包括Command 、 Decorator 、 Adapter 、 Strategy 、 Proxy 、 Chain of Responsibility 和 Interpreter 等 GOF 模式，还包括作为设计模式基础的著名 SOLID 设计概念。

这也是一门实践性很强的课程，作者将在课程中演示如何使用 IntelliJ IDEA 应用某种设计模式。

由于大多数演示都是单个文件，您可以下载与课程相关的文件并使用Intellij IDEA 、Eclipse、NetBeans 或您选择的任何 IDE 运行它。

这门课程的唯一缺点是它有点混合印地语和英语，这对于以英语为母语的人来说可能无法理解，但它使其成为同时了解印地语和英语的印度学习者的最佳课程。

如果您是 JavaScript 或 TypeScript 开发人员，正在寻找免费课程来了解面向对象的设计模式，那么本课程适合您。 本课程讨论 GOF 设计模式的主题，他出色地描述了每种模式的工作原理以及如何在代码中利用它来创建更好、更灵活的软件。

他的例子是真实的并且高度相关，这使得理解这些原则更容易。 例如，利用策略模式开发支付处理引擎是一个绝妙的主意，因为您需要处理各种格式的支付，例如现金、信用卡、优惠券、比特币或其他完全不同的形式。

简而言之，对于经验丰富的 Java 开发人员和任何有兴趣了解 GOF 设计模式（例如 Visitor、 Adapter和Decorator）的人来说，这是一次极好的培训。 本课程的唯一缺点是并未涵盖所有模式，但涵盖的内容都很好，您会更好地理解它们。

简而言之，学习设计模式的最佳课程之一是 TypeScript，而且它是完全免费的。

这只是另一门内容丰富的课程，它将教您来自著名的GOF 书中的 24 种面向对象的设计模式。

导师们非常称职，之前曾在谷歌和投资机构工作过。 他们认识到设计模式乍一看本质上并不简单，他们竭尽全力以一种可以理解的方式将它们分解。

与其他两个课程一样，本课程涵盖 OOP 设计概念和设计模式。 简而言之，这是学习 Java 设计模式并掌握全部 24 种模式以构建更好代码的绝佳课程。

The term "Design pattern" comes into play here. Design patterns, in basic words, are tried-and-true solutions to common programming problems. For instance, creational design patterns address object creation issues.

They've been around for a long time, but Erich Gamma, John Vlissides, Ralph Johnson, and Richard Helm's classic 1994 book Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software, often known as GOF design patterns, popularized them.

The design patterns were first introduced in C++ and Smalltalk, but they have subsequently been adopted by practically every programming language, including Java, C++, PHP, and even computer languages that aren't strictly object-oriented, such as JavaScript.

Items.Title: 适合大规模应用吗？ | Is CDN Right for Your Rails App? and good for large scale applications?
Items.Description:
随着网站变得越来越复杂和内容繁多，页面加载时间已成为影响用户体验的关键因素。 加快页面加载时间的一种解决方案是使用内容分发网络 (CDN)。 在这篇博文中，我们将讨论什么是 CDN、为什么它很重要，以及您是否应该在 Rails 应用程序中使用它。

什么是 CDN？

当用户从您的应用程序请求网页时，CDN 将从距离用户最近的服务器提供资产，从而减少交付内容所需的时间。

为什么要使用 CDN？

使用 CDN 有几个好处：

更快的页面加载时间

通过从离用户较近的服务器提供内容，CDN 可以显着减少网站加载所需的时间。 这对于远离应用程序服务器的用户尤为重要。

减少服务器负载

使用 CDN 时，应用程序的服务器不必提供静态资产，这有助于减少服务器负载并提高整体性能。

提高可用性

提高安全性

许多 CDN 提供额外的安全功能，例如 DDoS 保护和 SSL 证书，可以帮助保护您的网站免受攻击。

你应该在 Rails 中使用 CDN 吗？

是否应该在 Rails 7 应用程序中使用 CDN 取决于几个因素：

您的应用程序的大小
如果您的应用程序相对较小并且没有很多静态资产，则 CDN 可能不会提供太多好处。

您的用户的地理分布
如果您的应用程序的用户遍布世界各地，则 CDN 可以帮助确保您的网站能够为每个人快速加载。

成本
在做出决定之前，请务必权衡 CDN 的成本与潜在收益。

安全
如果安全是一个问题，CDN 可以为您的网站提供额外的保护。

如何在 Rails 中使用 CDN

如果您决定在 Rails 7 应用程序中使用 CDN，您可以通过将 Web 服务器配置为从 CDN 服务器提供静态资产来实现。 您需要向 CDN 提供资产的 URL，CDN 将处理其余部分。

配置资产主机

在您的config/application.rb文件中，您可以将config.asset_host variable设置为您的 CDN 的 URL。 例如：

 config.asset_host = 'https://cdn.example.com'

这将导致您的所有资产标签都使用 CDN 的 URL。

配置 Rails 为资产提供服务

如果您使用默认的 Rails 资产管道，则需要修改 Web 服务器的配置以从 CDN 的服务器为您的资产提供服务。 如果您使用的是 Nginx 或 Apache 之类的 Web 服务器，则可以通过添加以下配置将其配置为从 CDN 提供资源：

 location ~ ^/assets/ { expires 1y; add_header Cache-Control public; # Set the CDN as the asset host proxy_set_header Host cdn.example.com; # Serve assets from the CDN proxy_pass https://cdn.example.com; }

结论

总之，在 Rails 7 中使用 CDN 是提高 Web 应用程序性能的好方法。 但是，在做出决定之前考虑潜在的缺点（例如额外的复杂性和成本）很重要。



原文:

As websites have become more complex and content-heavy, page load time has become a critical factor in user experience. One solution to speed up page load times is to use a Content Delivery Network (CDN). In this blog post, we'll discuss what a CDN is, why it's important, and whether you should use one in your Rails application.

What is a CDN?

A CDN is a network of servers distributed around the world that store cached versions of your website's static assets, such as images, JavaScript, and CSS files. When a user requests a webpage from your application, the CDN will serve the assets from the server that is closest to the user, reducing the time it takes for the content to be delivered.

Why use a CDN?

There are several benefits to using a CDN:

Faster Page Load Times

By serving content from a server that is closer to the user, CDNs can dramatically reduce the time it takes for your website to load. This is especially important for users who are located far away from your application's server.

Reduced Server Load

When you use a CDN, your application's server doesn't have to serve static assets, which can help reduce server load and improve overall performance.

Increased Availability

CDNs are designed to handle high volumes of traffic, so they can help ensure that your website remains available during periods of high demand.

Improved Security

Many CDNs offer additional security features, such as DDoS protection and SSL certificates, that can help protect your website from attacks.

Should You Use a CDN in Rails?

Whether you should use a CDN in your Rails 7 application depends on a few factors:

Size of Your Application
If your application is relatively small and doesn't have a lot of static assets, a CDN might not provide much benefit.

Geographical Distribution of Your Users
If your application has users located all over the world, a CDN can help ensure that your website loads quickly for everyone.

Cost
CDNs can be expensive, especially for smaller applications. Be sure to weigh the cost of a CDN against the potential benefits before making a decision.

Security
If security is a concern, a CDN can offer additional protection for your website.

How to Use a CDN in Rails

If you decide to use a CDN in your Rails 7 application, you can do so by configuring your web server to serve your static assets from the CDN's server. You'll need to provide the CDN with the URL for your assets, and the CDN will handle the rest.

Configuring the Asset Host

In your config/application.rb file, you can set the config.asset_host variable to the URL for your CDN. For example:

config.asset_host = 'https://cdn.example.com'

This will cause all of your asset tags to use the CDN's URL.

Configuring Rails to Serve Assets

If you're using the default Rails asset pipeline, you'll need to modify your web server's configuration to serve your assets from the CDN's server. If you're using a web server like Nginx or Apache, you can configure it to serve assets from the CDN by adding the following configuration:

location ~ ^/assets/ {
  expires 1y;
  add_header Cache-Control public;

  # Set the CDN as the asset host
  proxy_set_header Host cdn.example.com;

  # Serve assets from the CDN
  proxy_pass https://cdn.example.com;
}

Conclusion

In conclusion, using a CDN in Rails 7 can be a great way to improve the performance of your web application. However, it's important to consider the potential downsides (such as additional complexity and cost) before making a decision.
